claims.evidence_text: A healthy wheat crop takes up 15 lbs/ac of phosphorus (P205 equivalent) in the first 30 days of growth, compared with soybeans that require only 1 lb/ac of phosphorus in the first 40 days of growth.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat takes up 15 lb/ac P2O5 in the first 30 days of growth (high early P demand)

claims.evidence_text: Phosphorus is less available in cool soils than in warm soils, with soil available phosphorus 5 times greater in 25 C soil than in 5 C. Cool soils result in less root growth, which further limits phosphorus uptake.
claims.claim_summary: Phosphorus availability for wheat is ~5x greater at 25 C soil than 5 C; cool fall soils limit uptake
